entries.position_summary: The United States highlights the IAEA’s key role in advancing peaceful uses under NPT Article IV and linking them to the UN Sustainable Development Goals, and seeks broader support for flagship IAEA initiatives (Rays of Hope, ZODIAC, plastics pollution). It underscores its leadership as the largest contributor to IAEA peaceful uses programs, noting over $630 million in voluntary support since 2010, including substantial funding for the Peaceful Uses Initiative and the Technical Cooperation Fund.
entries.quote: In this effort, we seek to draw more attention to the IAEA’s critical role in disseminating the benefits of peaceful uses of nuclear science and technology and making a more definitive link between the benefits of peaceful uses derived from the NPT Article IV and the UN’s Sustainable Development Goals. We aim to build support for the flagship initiatives of the IAEA, such as “Rays of Hope” on cancer, the Zoonotic Disease Integrated ACtion project, and Nuclear Technology for Controlling Plastics Pollution. The United States is proud to continue to be the largest contributor to IAEA peaceful uses activities. Since 2010, we have provided voluntary contributions totaling over $630 million to the Agency’s work to promote peaceful uses, including over $150 million to the Peaceful Uses Initiative, and over $320 million to the IAEA’s Technical Cooperation Fund.

entries.position_summary: The United States emphasizes the broad and growing benefits of peaceful nuclear applications beyond power and underscores its leadership in developing and exporting such technologies. It cites contributions such as advanced radiotherapy and the sterile insect technique to improve global health, agriculture, and related sectors.
entries.quote: Chair, The peaceful uses of nuclear science and technology are not limited to nuclear power. Since the NPT came into force over 50 years ago, the breadth of peaceful uses has grown tremendously, encompassing health, food, water, and more. The United States has been a proud leader in this journey. From the development of advanced radiotherapy techniques to diagnose and treat cancer to controlling harmful insect populations around the world with the sterile insect technique, the United States has pioneered technologies and exported them to improve the lives of millions of people worldwide.

entries.position_summary: The United States reaffirms its commitment to broaden NPT States Parties’ access to peaceful nuclear technologies—particularly those that help address climate change—and to foster cooperation with other States Parties and relevant international organizations, with a focus on supporting developing countries.
entries.quote: Finally, I would like to say a few words about the role of nuclear science and technology in tackling a global challenge that affects each of our countries – climate change. The United States has acknowledged again and again the need for urgent action to mitigate the causes of climate change and adapt to its impacts. We are particularly concerned in this regard for countries in the developing world where new, dangerous weather patterns threaten agricultural production and national economic well-being. In this regard, I wish to reaffirm our commitment to broadening access of NPT States Parties to peaceful uses, including technologies that help combat climate change, and to fostering cooperation with other States Parties and relevant international organizations to that end.

entries.position_summary: The United States emphasizes responsible, sustainable peaceful nuclear uses under the highest safety and security standards and highlights the IAEA’s central role in setting and supporting implementation of these norms. It urges all NPT States Parties to ratify and implement international nuclear safety and security treaties and commitments from the design phase onward.
entries.quote: The peaceful uses of the atom must be leveraged responsibly and under the highest standards of nuclear safety and security. The United States continues to prioritize implementing and maintaining these standards because they are essential for sustainability. Safe and secure nuclear technology is technology that citizens can trust, businesses can sell, utilities can rely on, and governments can back. The IAEA plays a critical role here as well by promoting safety standards and security guidance that reflect international consensus and supporting national regulatory bodies to implement these guidelines. We encourage all NPT States Parties to ratify and implement the international treaties and commitments related to nuclear safety and security throughout national planning – beginning with the design phase.

entries.position_summary: The country expresses support for the peaceful applications of nuclear energy, science, and technology. It notes and welcomes the broad, strong backing for these peaceful uses.
entries.quote: Finally, we were pleased to hear strong and widespread support for the numerous peaceful applications of nuclear energy, science, and technology.

entries.position_summary: Affirms the NPT’s inalienable right to peaceful nuclear uses, emphasizing benefits in energy, health, and agriculture, contingent on compliance with treaty obligations. Signals U.S. intent to promote and expand peaceful nuclear technologies, citing national efforts to strengthen the American nuclear enterprise.
entries.quote: Cluster III Statement (Peaceful Uses) NPT PrepCom 2025 Thank you Chair, The Nuclear Nonproliferation Treaty enshrines the “inalienable right” of all States Parties to leverage nuclear energy for peaceful purposes in conformity with their obligations under the Treaty so that we may leverage the peaceful atom in areas of energy, health, and agriculture. President Eisenhower envisioned this potential for nuclear science and technology in his Atoms for Peace speech over 70 years ago. Today, President Trump is furthering these efforts by unleashing the ingenuity and strength of the American nuclear enterprise.

entries.position_summary: The United States underscores the IAEA’s key role in enabling peaceful uses of nuclear science and technology, noting it has provided over $690 million in extra-budgetary support since 2010. It urges other States to increase support to the IAEA to advance shared NPT commitments.
entries.quote: The IAEA is the global clearinghouse in providing technical support and capacity building to Member States for the peaceful use of nuclear science and technology. Since 2010, the United States has provided over $690 million – the single largest contribution – in extra- budgetary funding and in-kind contributions to help the IAEA respond to the priorities of Member States. We call on States in a position to do so and who value this work to increase their support to the Agency to help us realize our shared commitment to the NPT.

entries.position_summary: Supports establishing a cooperative framework to promote peaceful uses of nuclear energy, science, and technology. Emphasizes facilitation of collaboration and access under the NPT’s peaceful uses pillar.
entries.quote: creating a framework that enables cooperation on the peaceful uses of nuclear energy, science, and technology.

entries.position_summary: The United States emphasizes strong support for NPT States Parties to access and benefit from peaceful applications of nuclear energy, science, and technology. It affirms a firm commitment to the NPT’s objectives, particularly the full realization of peaceful uses.
entries.quote: In the past year, the United States has continued its extraordinary efforts to support NPT States Parties seeking to benefit from the peaceful applications of nuclear energy, science, and technology in accordance with the Treaty. The United States is profoundly committed to the provisions and purposes of the NPT, including the full realization of the benefits of peaceful uses.

entries.position_summary: The United States highlights the role of nuclear science and technology in advancing sustainable development, including climate, food, water, and health goals. It supports expanding access to peaceful uses assistance to help achieve the UN SDGs.
entries.quote: At the 2000 NPT Review Conference, NPT States Parties recognized, “the importance of the concept of sustainable development as a guiding principle for the peaceful uses of nuclear energy.” The United States emphasizes that innovative, science-based solutions are needed to address global challenges in climate change, food and clean water, and protecting human and animal health. Nuclear science and technology advances are poised to help advance progress towards the UN Sustainable Development Goals, or SDGs, and we are committed to doing more to overcome barriers to accessing peaceful uses assistance.

entries.position_summary: The United States, with the United Kingdom, promotes the Sustained Dialogue on Peaceful Uses to expand collaboration and build lasting institutional links that advance peaceful nuclear applications in support of the UN SDGs. They acknowledge host countries’ contributions, invite States Parties to associate with Working Paper 38, and welcome proposals for new activities.
entries.quote: At the NPT Review Conference in 2022, the United States, with the United Kingdom, launched the Sustained Dialogue on Peaceful Uses to expand collaboration in this area. The initiative seeks to create institutional, long- lasting connections between the scientific and development communities. It has been a privilege to work with a broad group of experts and institutions to build greater awareness of how peaceful uses can help to achieve the UN SDGs. We recognize the contributions of Nigeria, Nepal, Thailand, and Paraguay in hosting activities under the Sustained Dialogue since the last Preparatory Committee Meeting. We welcome all NPT States Parties wishing to be associated with Working Paper 38 that describes the objectives and activities of the Sustained Dialogue and thank all delegations that joined our side event on July 25. We welcome ideas for new activities to promote peaceful uses.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes strong support for IAEA-led peaceful uses, exceeding its commitments to the Peaceful Uses Initiative with over $160 million to PUI and more than $690 million to IAEA peaceful uses since 2010, and urges other NPT States Parties to contribute extrabudgetary funds. It also endorses leveraging the upcoming IAEA ministerial and the Technical Cooperation Program to advance links between peaceful uses and the UN SDGs.
entries.quote: The Sustained Dialogue complements and amplifies the premier role of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) in facilitating peaceful uses. We plan to continue our support of IAEA initiatives to bolster the peaceful uses work of the Agency, especially to meet the needs of the least developed countries. We have met and exceeded our commitment to supporting the IAEA’s Peaceful Uses Initiative, or PUI. Our total contribution since the PUI was launched in 2010 has now exceeded 160 million dollars, and our contribution to IAEA peaceful uses work since 2010 totals over 690 million dollars. We encourage other NPT States Parties to make extrabudgetary contributions to IAEA peaceful uses activities through the PUI. We are looking forward to the IAEA’s upcoming Ministerial Conference on Nuclear Science, Technology and Applications and the Technical Cooperation Program as an opportunity to further advance the connection between peaceful uses and the UN SDGs.

entries.position_summary: The country welcomes the FAO–IAEA Atoms4Food initiative and plans to integrate it with national programs, signaling strong support for using nuclear science and techniques to enhance agricultural resilience and food security.
entries.quote: We note the joint launch by the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) and the IAEA of the new initiative “Atoms4Food.” At the national level, we are working to integrate Atoms4Food plans and activities with our national programs, including the our “Feed the Future” initiative and our “Vision for Adapted Crops and Soils,” that seeks to build a resilient food system grounded in nutritious, climate-adapted crops grown in fertile soils.

entries.position_summary: The United States promotes nuclear energy as a key tool for climate mitigation, collaborating with the IAEA on advanced reactors and SMRs, exploring non-electric applications, and providing infrastructure assistance to countries pursuing nuclear power, including support for related IAEA activities.
entries.quote: On climate change mitigation, the United States was proud to join with other countries in announcing the Declaration to Triple Nuclear Energy at COP28 in Dubai. The Declaration recognizes nuclear energy’s important role in achieving global net-zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2050 and highlights key steps to make this achievable. Our collaboration with the IAEA in next-generation nuclear technologies, for instance, supports the development and future deployment of advanced nuclear reactors and small modular reactors. We are also considering industrial and other non- electric applications for small modular reactors. We continue to provide infrastructure development assistance to countries that choose to pursue nuclear power to meet their climate and economic goals, including through support to IAEA activities in this area.

entries.position_summary: The United States underscores strong support for peaceful nuclear applications by funding and providing expertise to the IAEA’s Rays of Hope initiative and hosting a forum to expand access to cancer care in Africa. It is coordinating with African health ministries, the IAEA, and financial institutions to address the region’s growing cancer burden.
entries.quote: The United States continues to support the IAEA Rays of Hope initiative with substantial extrabudgetary contributions and expert support. We hosted the White House Africa Cancer Care Forum in Washington, D.C., in July 2024 to underscore the U.S. commitment to enhancing access to cancer care in Africa. Representatives of the Ministries of Health from African countries met with cancer experts, the IAEA, and international financial institutions to identify ways to meet the growing health care burden of cancer in the region.

entries.position_summary: The United States underscores nuclear technology’s vital role in addressing sustainable development challenges and supports the IAEA’s efforts on peaceful uses. It promotes initiatives like the Sustained Dialogue on Peaceful Uses and efforts to make nuclear power more affordable to advance the SDGs.
entries.quote: Its efforts are also needed to address urgent sustainable development challenges, from climate change to energy, water, food, and health insecurity. As IAEA Director General Grossi aptly put it, we now “have the means to avoid the worst and to adapt to new realities—with nuclear technology a vital part of the solution.” We strongly agree. And for that reason, the United States continues to support the IAEA in these efforts, and to create new opportunities to advance the Sustainable Development Goals, such as through the Sustained Dialogue on Peaceful Uses and the development of more affordable nuclear power.

entries.position_summary: Advocates expanding equitable access for all NPT Parties to peaceful nuclear applications and highlights their contributions to achieving the UN Sustainable Development Goals.
entries.quote: It also means doing more to ensure that all Parties can access the benefits of peaceful uses. We can start by recognizing the numerous ways the peaceful application of nuclear science and technology can help advance the UN Sustainable Development Goals.

entries.position_summary: The United States emphasizes improving global access to peaceful nuclear science and technology, noting disparities in access. It highlights ongoing support for bilateral and IAEA peaceful uses projects and the launch of the U.S.-UK ‘Sustained Dialogue on Peaceful Uses’ initiative to advance this goal.
entries.quote: We are aware that not all countries are equally able to access those benefits, and the United States is committed to doing even more to improve access to the peaceful uses of nuclear science and technology. In addition to our numerous contributions to bilateral and IAEA peaceful uses projects, last year, at the NPT Review Conference, together with our partners in the United Kingdom, we announced a new initiative called the “Sustained Dialogue on Peaceful Uses.” This initiative was shaped by the advice of a wide range of global experts.

entries.position_summary: The United States emphasizes the importance of nuclear power for combating climate change and addressing energy needs in developing countries. It commits to leading the development and deployment of advanced nuclear reactor technologies, including through international collaboration.
entries.quote: In addition, the United States believes nuclear power plays a crucial role in addressing the challenges of climate change and addressing energy deficits in developing countries. We are committed to continuing to lead the development of new nuclear power technologies, including our collaboration on next- generation nuclear technologies for the future deployment of advanced reactors.

entries.position_summary: The country argues that robust export controls, alongside UNSCR 1540 measures, are essential to enable responsible international nuclear cooperation for peaceful purposes. It rejects claims that export controls hinder peaceful uses, asserting they prevent diversion, ensure peaceful end-use, and support economic development.
entries.quote: We also affirm that export controls, in addition to the tools advanced through UNSCR 1540, are necessary enablers for the responsible international exchange of nuclear technology for peaceful uses. We therefore reject efforts by some Parties to incorrectly imply that they are not. Export controls prevent diversion of sensitive technologies, ensure technology exported for peaceful uses remains in peaceful uses, and actually contribute to economic development.

entries.position_summary: The country argues that strategic trade controls and multilateral export control regimes are consistent with, and actually bolster confidence in, the NPT right to peaceful nuclear uses. It contends these controls do not impede international technology exchange or economic development.
entries.quote: consistent with, and in fact facilitate and enhance confidence in, the right of States Parties to develop and use nuclear energy and technology for peaceful purposes. Though there is ample rhetoric to the contrary, there is no evidence that strategic trade controls, including the multilateral export control regimes, hamper the international exchange of technology for peaceful uses or hinder any country’s economic development.

entries.position_summary: The United States, in cooperation with the IAEA, is launching an International School on Peaceful Uses to train developing countries in responsible civilian nuclear applications. This initiative aims to build capacity across sectors like health, agriculture, water, environment, waste management, and industry, fostering global partnerships and sustainable development. It underscores the U.S. commitment to promoting peaceful nuclear technologies through education and collaboration.
entries.quote: The United States, in cooperation with the IAEA, has also developed an International School on Peaceful Uses of Nuclear Applications, an educational and collaborative initiative for developing countries to advance understanding and applications of nuclear technologies for peaceful purposes, including how nuclear technology can be responsibly used for socio-economic development. The School will address various applications for peaceful uses, including radiopharmaceuticals, agriculture and food security, water management and isotope hydrology, environmental monitoring and waste management, and industrial applications and non- destructive testing. The pilot program will take place this fall in the United States. The School is not only a platform for knowledge dissemination but also a catalyst for fostering global partnerships and advancing scientific and technological progress. This initiative underscores the U.S. commitment to promoting the peaceful uses of nuclear technology, which reinforces international cooperation for sustainable development.

entries.position_summary: The United States commits significant voluntary funding and technical assistance to enable safe and secure access to peaceful nuclear energy, including hundreds of programs on safeguards, nuclear security, and export controls. It reports providing over a quarter-billion dollars in extra-budgetary support to IAEA safeguards since 2015 and expresses a strong commitment to expanding peaceful uses.
entries.quote: In each of these areas, the United States devotes significant financial resources, technology, training and policy assistance on a voluntary basis to assure the fullest, secure and safe access to peaceful nuclear energy and technology. This includes hundreds of support programs conducted worldwide relating to nuclear safeguards, nuclear security, and export controls, as well as more than a quarter of a billion dollars of extrabudgetary IAEA safeguards support since the 2015 Review Conference. The United States is deeply committed to improving access to peaceful uses, and we look forward to discussing this further in our Cluster III statement.

entries.position_summary: The United States supports the peaceful applications of nuclear technology, backing the IAEA’s GloWAL initiative to use isotope hydrology for water resource management and participating actively in its development. It also promotes equitable, climate-resilient water and sanitation infrastructure domestically and globally.
entries.quote: In 2023, on World Water Day, the United States announced support to global and domestic actions to promote equitable access and climate- resilient water and sanitation infrastructure. In parallel, the United States supported the IAEA’s launch of its Global Water Analysis Laboratory network (GloWAL), which aims to assist countries in using isotope hydrology to collect data used in making decisions on water resource management, including addressing water scarcity and improving water quality. We participated actively in the first consultancy meeting for the GloWAL network in June 2024.

entries.position_summary: The country supports the U.S.-UK Sustained Dialogue on Peaceful Uses initiative, has submitted a working paper on it, and intends to engage further through a side event. It is actively promoting cooperation on peaceful nuclear applications.
entries.quote: This idea is what lies behind the U.S and UK initiative called the Sustained Dialogue on Peaceful Uses. We have submitted a Working Paper on this Dialogue and look forward to discussing it further in our side event.

entries.position_summary: The country urges action to prevent abuse of Article X and to adopt principles that deter NPT withdrawals. It argues that withdrawal undermines universality, does not erase responsibility for pre-withdrawal noncompliance, and does not diminish obligations regarding the use or misuse of peaceful nuclear cooperation.
entries.quote: Chair, It is long past time that States Parties address the challenge of abuse of the NPT’s withdrawal right in Article X. Since the DPRK’s announced withdrawal in 2003, four Review Conferences have been unable to reach consensus on ways to discourage similar action by others in the future. NPT States Parties can make a start by acknowledging several basic principles: First, withdrawal from the NPT by definition sets back the goal of universality. Second, it must be recognized that a withdrawing state remains responsible under international law for any unresolved noncompliance prior to withdrawal. And third, a States Party’s withdrawal in no way minimizes its obligations regarding the use or misuse the fruits of peaceful nuclear cooperation.

entries.position_summary: The United States supports international cooperation on small modular reactors through capacity building and infrastructure assistance, integrating safeguards and security by design. It also backs the IAEA’s Milestones Approach to ensure new nuclear power programs meet the highest standards of safety, security, and safeguards.
entries.quote: In this regard, I note that the United States supports a range of bilateral and regional assistance to countries seeking to cooperate with the United States on small modular reactor technology, including through capacity building programs, infrastructure assistance, and integrating safeguards and security into new reactor designs. These activities are supplemental to our support for IAEA activities, including the Agency’s Milestones Approach, which enables a sound development process for a national nuclear power program that prioritizes the highest standards of safety, security, and safeguards.

entries.position_summary: The country calls for using the NPT review process to advance nuclear restraint through renewed arms control and concrete risk-reduction measures. It insists on full compliance with IAEA safeguards and supports expanding peaceful nuclear uses globally.
entries.quote: At a time of rising instability and risk, let’s use this review process to advance policies and practices of nuclear restraint, including action on arms control and risk reduction, insisting on full compliance with IAEA safeguards, and facilitating an expansion of peaceful uses on a global scale.

entries.position_summary: The country endorses the IAEA’s central role in supporting states that are starting or expanding nuclear power programs, highlighting the Agency’s SMR Platform and Nuclear Harmonization and Standardization Initiative as key mechanisms. It emphasizes facilitating peaceful nuclear applications through IAEA assistance.
entries.quote: We reaffirm the essential role of the International Atomic Energy Agency in providing technical support to nations seeking to embark on or expand nuclear power programs, including through its Small Modular Reactor Platform and its Nuclear Harmonization and Standardization Initiative.

entries.position_summary: They emphasize the NPT’s value in enabling the fullest possible international cooperation on peaceful nuclear applications, signaling strong support for the Treaty’s peaceful uses pillar.
entries.quote: Chair, The NPT brings incredible value to its States Parties by providing a framework that facilitates the fullest possible international cooperation on the peaceful uses of nuclear

entries.position_summary: The United States highlights the NPT’s essential role in enabling international cooperation on the peaceful uses of nuclear energy, science, and technology. It expresses intent to continue working with NPT States Parties to advance these shared goals.
entries.quote: We appreciate the growing number of opportunities to work together to draw greater attention to the critical role of the NPT in providing a framework that facilitates the fullest possible international cooperation and collaboration in peaceful uses of nuclear energy, science, and technology. We aim to underscore and reinforce the many common interests and objectives we share as NPT States Parties and to find new ways to promote our shared aspirations. The United States looks forward to continuing to work together towards this end.

entries.position_summary: Emphasizes the importance of strong national regulatory bodies from the earliest planning stages to support peaceful nuclear applications. Highlights their crucial role in radiation safety and protection in medical uses, including calibration of nuclear and radiation medicine equipment.
entries.quote: The work of national regulatory bodies is critical to both power and non- power applications of nuclear science and technology and should be part of national planning from very early stages. For example, in the field of human health, an effective regulatory body has a crucial role in maintaining radiation safety guidelines, assessing institutional practices for radiation protection of both patients and workers, and calibrating nuclear and radiation medicine equipment and sources.

entries.position_summary: The United States advocates a global resurgence in civil nuclear energy and aims to lead international cooperation and deployment of advanced nuclear power. It emphasizes that such growth will occur under the highest standards of safety, security, and non-proliferation. The U.S. positions its technology as the safest and most secure for peaceful energy use.
entries.quote: Chair, Nowhere is this more evident than in civil nuclear energy. President Trump has committed the United States to lead a nuclear resurgence to advance our shared security and prosperity. Continued growth and innovation in nuclear power will decrease dependence on less reliable sources of energy, reduce costs for consumers, and increase economic investment opportunities. The United States has the safest, most secure, and most advanced nuclear technology on offer today and is positioned to lead in civil nuclear cooperation globally. For instance, we are working diligently and creatively to enable the rapid deployment of advanced nuclear energy that is intended to provide abundant and affordable energy under the highest standards of safety, security, and non-proliferation.

entries.position_summary: The country advocates institutionalizing UNODA as the permanent secretariat for the NPT review process. It also supports formalizing the IAEA’s supporting role—particularly on non-proliferation and peaceful uses—by staffing the secretariat for those issue areas.
entries.quote: • Formalizing the United Nations role by establishing the Office for Disarmament Affairs as the permanent secretariat for the Non-Proliferation Treaty review process rather than deciding separately for each Non-Proliferation Treaty review cycle. Such a decision could also formalize the supporting role of the International Atomic Energy Agency in staffing the secretariat for issues of non-proliferation and the peaceful uses of nuclear technology.

entries.position_summary: The United States argues that robust nuclear export controls, consistent with Article III, are essential to nonproliferation and actually facilitate legitimate nuclear trade in line with NPT objectives. It highlights ongoing U.S. cooperation to update NSG Guidelines and Control Lists to address evolving technologies and proliferation risks.
entries.quote: Nuclear export controls, including those required under Article III, are another essential nonproliferation measure, necessary to ensure that nuclear-related transfers do not inadvertently contribute to the development of nuclear weapons. I wish to emphasize that nuclear export controls do not impede legitimate nuclear trade; in fact, they enable it and ensure consistency with the NPT’s purposes. The United States has long been a leader in this area, and continues to work with partners to update NSG Guidelines and Control Lists so that they remain effective in the face of evolving trade and technological developments and proliferation concerns.

entries.position_summary: The country promotes advanced nuclear reactors for electricity and broader industrial/agricultural applications, emphasizing peaceful nuclear uses. It supports capacity-building to establish strong, independent national regulatory frameworks. It leads and assists internationally to enhance nuclear safety and security, including through bilateral cooperation.
entries.quote: generation nuclear technologies, for instance, supports the development and future deployment of advanced nuclear reactors for electricity but also for a range of industrial and agricultural needs. We conduct and support capacity-building activities to help countries develop robust, independent regulatory infrastructures. We lead international efforts in improving nuclear safety and security worldwide and to provide bilateral cooperation and assistance to help others do the same.

entries.position_summary: The United States emphasizes partnering with the IAEA and other States to expand peaceful nuclear technologies and energy worldwide while upholding stringent nonproliferation, safety, and security standards. It reaffirms the IAEA’s essential role in facilitating peaceful uses and preventing proliferation and commits to advancing domestic and international nuclear energy deployment under these high standards.
entries.quote: The United States partners with the IAEA and other States Parties to advance innovative breakthroughs in nuclear technologies and applications, while maintaining the highest standards of safety, security, and nonproliferation. We are applying our experience and expertise in a wide range of sectors, including providing abundant and affordable nuclear energy solutions and installing state-of-the-art technology at hospitals and research facilities around the world. We reaffirm the essential role of the IAEA in expanding access to peaceful uses while preventing nuclear proliferation. And we reiterate our commitment to unleashing a golden age of American energy, and to empowering partner countries worldwide to deploy nuclear energy – again, under the highest standards of nuclear security, safety, and nonproliferation.

entries.position_summary: The statement credits the NPT with containing proliferation, establishing robust IAEA safeguards, and advancing disarmament through stockpile reductions and arms control. It also praises the spread of nuclear-weapon-free zones and the benefits delivered by peaceful nuclear applications.
entries.quote: Since it entered into force, we have contained nuclear proliferation; developed robust international safeguards to monitor peaceful nuclear programs; made significant steps towards disarmament through stockpile reductions and other arms control measures; seen entire regions adopt nuclear-weapon-free zones; and, through the peaceful uses of nuclear energy, science, and technology, improved the lives of millions.

entries.position_summary: The United States supports bilateral cooperation on peaceful nuclear energy, citing an NCMOU with El Salvador aimed at enhancing energy security and mutual prosperity. It expresses readiness to work with responsible partners and pursue follow-on initiatives.
entries.quote: The United States stands ready and willing to work bilaterally with responsible partners. For example, we signed a Nuclear Cooperation Memorandum of Understanding – or NCMOU – with El Salvador in February with the aim of working as partners to enhance energy security and support mutual prosperity. We followed that NCMOU by launching a

entries.position_summary: The country advocates robust, responsible nuclear export controls as essential to nonproliferation and to enabling the fullest possible exchange for peaceful nuclear uses. It underscores the role of UNSCR 1540 in requiring domestic legal and regulatory measures to prevent nuclear terrorism, supporting strong national export control frameworks.
entries.quote: Chair, Responsible nuclear export controls are essential to effective nonproliferation and to facilitating the “fullest possible exchange” of nuclear material, equipment, and technology for peaceful purposes. Through Resolution 1540, export controls also help prevent nuclear terrorism by obligating States Parties to institute domestic legal- regulatory measures and controls. Robust export controls are thoroughly

entries.position_summary: The country signals intent to engage on arms control, nuclear risk reduction, and effective verification, prioritizes full compliance with IAEA safeguards, and supports expanding access to peaceful uses of nuclear technology ahead of the next Review Conference.
entries.quote: Ahead of next year’s Review Conference, there is much to be done. Over the next year, we look forward to engaging on ideas for arms control, reducing nuclear risk, and effective verification; on ensuring full compliance with IAEA safeguards; and on expanding access to peaceful uses. As Secretary of State Rubio has repeatedly said, “peace is not a noun, it is a verb.” So, let’s get to work.

entries.position_summary: The country underscores that robust nuclear security, alongside IAEA safeguards, is essential to prevent theft, trafficking, and misuse of nuclear materials and technologies. It calls for the highest standards of nuclear safety and security as peaceful nuclear applications and emerging technologies grow. It supports the IAEA’s work to uphold safety and security in Ukraine and condemns actions that jeopardize these efforts.
entries.quote: In addi�on to safeguards, nuclear security is cri�cally important to preven�ng prolifera�on. Ensuring nuclear facili�es are protected, and the materials and technologies therein are secure, prevents poten�al the�, trafficking, and misuse, including use to inflict significant harm. To that end, we must con�nue to priori�ze the highest standards of nuclear safety and security as peaceful uses of nuclear technology expand and countries pursue new and emerging technologies. We also reaffirm our support for the IAEA’s efforts to uphold nuclear safety and security in Ukraine and condemn ac�ons that jeopardize these efforts.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes the significant benefits of peaceful nuclear applications for national welfare and for advancing the UN 2030 Sustainable Development Goals, indicating strong support for promoting peaceful uses under the NPT.
entries.quote: More than fifty years after the NPT entered into force, we are aware of the potential for peaceful uses to make a significant contribution to a country’s national welfare and to achieving the goals under the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development. These benefits

entries.position_summary: The United States highlights its longstanding leadership in peaceful nuclear applications and commits to revitalizing nuclear energy under President Trump. It encourages international partners to join this effort to expand peaceful nuclear energy use.
entries.quote: energy, science, and technology. For over 70 years, the United States has led the development and application of peaceful uses. Now, under the leadership of President Trump, we commit to unleashing a golden age of American energy and ushering in a nuclear energy resurgence. We invite you to join the United States in this endeavor.

entries.position_summary: The United States states that clean, carbon-free nuclear energy is essential for addressing climate change and emphasizes being a leading contributor to international cooperation on civilian nuclear energy.
entries.quote: The United States understands that clean, carbon-free nuclear energy is an essential component to limiting the impacts of climate change, which is why we are among the world’s strongest contributors to civilian nuclear energy cooperation. Our collaboration in next-

entries.position_summary: The United States promotes global expansion of civil nuclear energy through programs like FIRST and numerous cooperation agreements, emphasizing deployment under the highest safety, security, and nonproliferation standards. It highlights extensive partnerships and instruments (civil-nuclear agreements, NCMOUs, and IGAs) supporting responsible nuclear energy development.
entries.quote: bilateral civil nuclear energy partnership with El Salvador under the Foundational Infrastructure for Responsible Use of Small Modular Reactor Technology or FIRST program. FIRST empowers partner countries worldwide to deploy nuclear energy under the highest nuclear security, safety, and nonproliferation standards. And we are doing more to partner around the world. Today the United States has: civil-nuclear cooperation Agreements in place with 50 partners; nine NCMOUs with countries across four continents; four Intergovernmental Agreements (IGAs) facilitating additional civil nuclear cooperation, and FIRST is actively engaged with 51 countries.

entries.position_summary: The United States underscores that peaceful nuclear applications depend on strong safety and security, endorsing the IAEA’s role in building regulatory capacity in developing countries. It prioritizes maintaining the highest safety and security standards and views a strong global safety record as essential for peaceful use.
entries.quote: The 2000 NPT Review Conference highlighted the role of the IAEA in assisting developing countries to improve regulatory capacity to support scientific and technical advances. It noted that, “…a demonstrated global record of safety is a key element for the peaceful uses of nuclear energy.” The United States continues to prioritize implementing and maintaining the highest standards of safety and security because such

entries.position_summary: The country notes that, despite broad interest in risk reduction and in linking peaceful uses of nuclear technology to the Sustainable Development Goals, the NPT Preparatory Committee has failed to reach consensus on substantive recommendations. This implies support for progress on these topics and concern about the functioning of the review process.
entries.quote: Even when there is a strong convergence of interest on certain topics, such as risk reduction or linking peaceful uses to Sustainable Development Goals, the Preparatory Committee has never been able to reach consensus on substantive recommendations on those or any other topics.

entries.position_summary: The United States praises the NPT’s success and urges States Parties to use the PrepCom to advance the Treaty through arms control, reducing risks of nuclear conflict, ensuring full compliance with IAEA safeguards, and expanding access to peaceful uses of nuclear technology.
entries.quote: Statement by the United States to the NPT Preparatory Committee Statement by Deputy Assistant Secretary of State Gonzalo Suarez, U.S. Department of State, Bureau of International Security and Nonproliferation New York, NY May 9, 2025 Chair, colleagues, In our opening statement last week, the United States praised the remarkable success of this Treaty over the past fifty-five years and reaffirmed that all States Parties continue to benefit greatly from it. We urged all States Parties to use this PrepCom to develop positive ideas that advance the NPT, whether on arms control, reducing the risk of nuclear conflict, ensuring full compliance with IAEA safeguards, or expanding access to peaceful uses.

entries.position_summary: The statement argues that Russia’s war against Ukraine endangers Ukrainian nuclear facilities, threatening safety, security, and safeguards, which could erode public trust in nuclear technology and undermine peaceful applications. It warns that such damage would harm global development and climate efforts and negatively impact the NPT, with responsibility attributed to Russia.
entries.quote: Global efforts to promote and support the growth of nuclear power with the highest standards of safety, security, and safeguards is, however, put at risk because of Russia’s war against Ukraine. Any damage to Ukrainian nuclear reactors would feed into public distrust of nuclear technology in general and challenge public acceptance of even the non-power applications of nuclear science and technology in areas like food safety, health, and water resource management. Damage to Ukrainian facilities would damage global development, damage efforts to address climate change, and damage the NPT – and that would be on Russia.

entries.position_summary: The statement endorses a strong NPT-based nonproliferation regime as essential for broad sharing of peaceful nuclear technology and as the foundation for advancing nuclear disarmament. It links peaceful uses with progress toward fulfilling disarmament commitments.
entries.quote: It is a fact that a strong nonproliferation regime, anchored in the NPT, makes possible the widespread sharing of the peaceful atom and is the basis for pursuit of steps toward nuclear disarmament.

entries.position_summary: The country urges NPT Parties to strengthen cooperation on peaceful nuclear uses, conditioned by robust safeguards. It supports bilateral obligations that embed such safeguards as a way to protect shared nonproliferation interests.
entries.quote: We encourage NPT Parties to consider actions that reinforce these elements, in particular in their cooperation on peaceful uses of nuclear energy and associated safeguards-related conditions. These bilateral obligations reinforce and protect the shared interests of all NPT Parties.

entries.position_summary: The country condemns Russia’s attacks and seizure of Ukraine’s nuclear facilities, asserting that these actions undermine Ukraine’s peaceful nuclear energy program and compromise nuclear safety and security. It frames the situation as a direct threat to the peaceful uses of nuclear technology in Ukraine.
entries.quote: Chair, Russia’s attacks against and seizure of Ukraine’s nuclear facilities directly undercut Ukraine’s ability to maintain its own peaceful nuclear energy program. Russia’s actions continue to compromise every one of

entries.position_summary: They advocate broad participation—especially by developing countries—in the Sustained Dialogue on Peaceful Uses (SDPU) and have submitted a working paper summarizing the SDPU’s activities over the past year.
entries.quote: support broad participation in the Dialogue’s activities, particularly by developing countries. We have submitted a working paper describing the SDPU’s work in the last year.

entries.position_summary: The country underscores that strong security for nuclear and radiological materials and facilities is essential to ensure they remain useful and exclusively for peaceful purposes.
entries.quote: Security of nuclear and radiological materials and facilities is essential to preserving their usefulness as well as their peacefulness.

entries.position_summary: The United States affirms the inalienable right to peaceful nuclear energy consistent with NPT Articles I and II, emphasizing nuclear power’s role in meeting global energy needs and positioning itself as a leading supplier. It stresses that NPT-facilitated cooperation should be contingent on adherence to the highest nonproliferation standards to ensure safety and security, implying robust safeguards and supply conditions.
entries.quote: The United States acknowledges the inalienable right to research, production and use of nuclear energy for peaceful purposes in conformity with NPT obligations under Articles I and II and we continue to emphasize that nuclear energy will significantly contribute to the world’s energy needs, with the United States ready to be the preeminent supplier. The NPT facilitates peaceful nuclear cooperation, and conforming with the highest nonproliferation standards will ensure this cooperation is safe and secure.

entries.position_summary: The United States emphasizes shared objectives among NPT States Parties and highlights the importance and benefits of peaceful uses of nuclear energy, science, and technology. It calls for cooperative engagement throughout the review cycle to advance these common goals.
entries.quote: Statement by the United States Cluster III General Statement Preparatory Committee Meeting for the 2026 Review Conference of the Parties to the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT) Delivered by Ambassador Laura SH Holgate Vienna, Austria August 8, 2023 Chair, Throughout this review cycle, the United States hopes that we can all remember the many common interests and objectives we share as NPT States Parties, and that we can work together to promote ways to achieve our shared aspirations. One of these common interests is the benefits we derive from the peaceful uses of nuclear energy, science, and technology.

entries.position_summary: The country argues that discouraging NPT withdrawal protects the collective interests of all Parties and stresses that leaving the Treaty does not relieve a state of separate obligations not to misuse peaceful nuclear cooperation. Withdrawal should not enable diversion of peaceful nuclear benefits to non-peaceful ends.
entries.quote: protects the mutual interests of all other NPT Parties. Furthermore, withdrawal from the NPT does not alter other obligations a withdrawing state may have, separate from the NPT, not to misuse the fruits of peaceful nuclear cooperation.
